ORDER AND STIPULATION FOR MOTION TO BE HEARD BY A MEMBER OF THE CALIFORNIA BAR SERVING AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF CHRISTIAN ROETTGERS' MOTION FOR SANCTIONS FOR AUTONET'S FAILURE TO APPEAR AT THREE PARTY WITNESSES DEPOSITIONS AND FOR IMPROPER INSTRUCTIONS AT THE CONTINUED DEPOSITION IS DENIED. THE RECORD CLEARLY REFLECTS PLAINTIFF HAD AGREED TO MOVE THE DEPOSITION DATES OF THE THREE PARTY WITNESSES UPON WHICH DEFENDANTS RELIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PROVIDE EVIDENCE THAT THE DEPOSITION OF MS. BONETTI WAS LEFT OPEN AS TO ANY AND ALL QUESTIONS; THE TRANSCRIPTS FAILS TO SET FORTH CLEARLY THE FULL REASON FOR A FURTHER DEPOSITION BUT IT IS CLEAR THERE WERE CERTAIN OBJECTIONS THAT WERE MADE THE BASIS FOR THE FURTHER DEPOSITION. FURTHER PLAINTIFF DID NOT PROVIDE A CRC 3.1345(A) STATEMENT AS REQUIRED. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: ARLENE BARTON; CLERK L. FONG; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

DEFENDANT EASTON CAPITAL PARTNERS LP'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MAY AMEND, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO PLEAD SPECIFIC ACTS COMMITTED BY AN EASTON CAPITAL PARTNERS LP OFFICER, DIRECTOR, OR MANAGING AGENT, AS REQUIRED BY CAL. CIV. CODE 3294(B). ORDER VACATING THE TRIAL DATE AND SETTING A CMC

MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON SEP-05-2013 IN 206, DEFENDANT EASTON CAPITAL PARTNERS, L.P.'S MOTION TO RESET OR CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT VACATED THE TRIAL DATE OF 11/18/13 AND SET THE MATTER TO CASE MANAGMENT CONFERENCE OF 11/6/13 AT 10:30AM IN DEPT. 610 FOR CASE TO BE AT ISSUE. NOTICE TO BE MAILED BY THE COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CYNTHIA M. LEE; CLERK: RVELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT EASTON CAPITAL PARTNERS LP'S DEMURRER TO THE TENTH AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION OF THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. EASTON CAPITAL PARTNERS LP IS NOT AN AGENT OF AUTONET MOBILE INC. UNDER CALIFORNIA AGENCY LAWS, AND THE BUSINESS JUDGMENT RULE IS INAPPLICABLE AS EASTON CAPITAL PARTNERS LP IS NOT A DIRECTOR OF AUTONET MOBILE INC. (CAL. CORP. CODE 309.) THE PLEADINGS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE THAT EASTON CAPITAL PARTNERS LP INTENTIONALLY ACTED TO INDUCE A BREACH OF CONTRACT AND INTENTIONALLY INTERFERE WITH A CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP. PLEADINGS ALSO STATE THAT EASTON CAPITAL PARTNERS LP'S CONDUCT CAUSED THE BREACH AND DISRUPTION OF THE CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION SCHEDULED FOR APRIL 18 IS OFF CALENDAR. PLAINTIFF SHALL IMMEDIATELY FILE AND SERVE THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. DISCOVERY REMAINS OPEN AND DEFENDANT MAY BRING A NEW MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. IF THE NOVEMBER 18, 2013 TRIAL DATE IS NOT MUTUALLY AGREEABLE, THE PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER BEFORE THE HEARING AND BE PREPARED TO PROPOSE AN ALTERNATE DATE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDERQ FILED BY DEFENDANT AUTONET MOBILE, INC. HEARING SET FOR APR-23-2013 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT AUTONET MOBILE, INC.'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. THIS MATTER IS PRESENTLY SET FOR TRIAL ON MAY 20, 2013 AND PLAINTIFF HAS THE RIGHT TO COMPLETE THE TAKING OF HIS DEPOSITION TO PREPARE FOR TRIAL. THERE HAS BEEN NO SHOWING OF GOOD CAUSE AS REQUIRED BY SECTION 2025.420(B) TO JUSTIFY THE ISSUANCE OF A PROTECTIVE ORDER. MR. PRATZ HAS HAD ALMOST ONE MONTH TO OBTAIN SEPARATE COUNSEL IF HE HAD SO CHOSEN TO DO SO AND HIS FAILURE TO DO SO IS NOT A BASIS FOR DENYING PLAINTIFF THE RIGHT TO COMPLETE THE DEPOSITION. IF THE COURT GRANT'S PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT AND ADD MR. PRATZ AS A NAMED DEFENDANT UNDOUBTEDLY THE PRESENT TRIAL DATE WILL BE VACATED AND COUNSEL SHOULD BE ABLE TO WORK OUT A SCHEDULE THAT WILL ACCOMMODATE THE INTERESTS OF ALL PARTIES TO THIS LITIGATION. THE COURT WILL NOT IMPOSE ANY MONETARY SANCTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PAUL RENNE, JUDGE PRO-TEM, CYNTHIA HERBERT, CLERK, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT AUTONET MOBILE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, THIRD, AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND SUSTAINED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. THE GENERAL DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED AS PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED THE ELEMENTS OF A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WAGE VIOLATIONS UNDER LABOR CODE SECTIONS 201 AND 202. THE GENERAL DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO PROVIDE ACCURATE WAGE STATEMENTS UNDER LABOR CODE SECTION 226 IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN 15 DAYS TO PLEAD FACTS, IN GOOD FAITH, THAT DEMONSTRATE AN INJURY AROSE FROM THE MISSING/INACCURATE INFORMATION IN AUTONET'S WAGE STATEMENTS. SEE PRICE V. STARBUCKS CORP, 192 CAL. APP. 4TH 1136, 1142-43 (2011). THE GENERAL DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE SECTION 17200 ET SEQ. IS OVERRULED AS PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED FACTS REGARDING AUTONET'S ALLEGED UNFAIR AND UNLAWFUL BUSINESS PRACTICES. SEE KHOURY V. MALY'S OF CALIFORNIA, INC., 14 CAL. APP. 4TH 612, 619, (1993). THE GENERAL DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND/OR BREACH OF COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS THAT AUTONET BREACHED ITS ORAL PROMISE TO PLAINTIFF TO REPAY HIS DEFERRED SALARY IF HE CONTINUED TO WORK FOR AUTONET ON A REDUCED SALARY THROUGH ITS LIQUIDITY CRISIS. (FAC 8, 25.) PLAINTIFF'S SPECIAL DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, THIRD, AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS NOT REQUIRED TO ALLEGE THE EXISTENCE OF A CONTRACT AT THE PLEADING STAGE TO OVERCOME A DEMURRER. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, IT CAN BE ASCERTAINED FROM THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT THE ALLEGED CONTRACT BETWEEN ROETTGERS AND AUTONET WAS AN ORAL AGREEMENT. (FAC 8, 25.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MA

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-26-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. BARBARA GIUFFRE OF WORK/ENVIRONMENT LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 981-9114 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DOUG COLT OF COLT/WALLERSTEIN, TEL. NO. (650) 453-1981 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EASTON CAPITAL PARTNERS LP'S DEMURRER TO THE TENTH AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION OF THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled as to both causes of action. Easton Capital Partners LP is not an agent of Autonet Mobile Inc. under California agency laws, and the business judgment rule is inapplicable as Easton Capital Partners LP is not a director of Autonet Mobile Inc. (Cal. Corp. Code 309.) The pleadings sufficiently allege that Easton Capital Partners LP intentionally acted to induce a breach of contract and intentionally interfere with a contractual relationship. Pleadings also state that Easton Capital Partners LP's conduct caused the breach and disruption of the contractual relationship. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)

ATTY. DOUG COLT OF COLT/WALLERSTEIN, TEL. NO. (650) 453-1981 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EASTON CAPITAL PARTNERS LP'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted, with leave to amend. Plaintiff may amend, if he can do so in good faith, to plead specific acts committed by an Easton Capital Partners LP officer, director, or managing agent, as required by Cal. Civ. Code 3294(B). Plaintiff has otherwise pled malice and/or oppression for the purposes of establishing a punitive damages claim. Defendant to prepare order. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BARBARA GIUFFRE OF WROK ENVIRONMENT LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 981-9114 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOHN L. FITZGERALD OF STRAZULO/FITZGERLAD LLP, TEL. NO. 394-9500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. DOUG COLT OF COLT/WALLERSTEIN LLP, TEL. NO. (650) 453-1981 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AUTONET MOBILE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled as to the first, third, and fourth causes of action, and sustained as to the second cause of action. The general demurrer to the first cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has sufficiently pled the elements of a cause of action for wage violations under Labor Code Sections 201 and 202. The general demurrer to the second cause of action for failure to provide accurate wage statements under Labor Code Section 226 is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given 15 days to plead facts, in good faith, that demonstrate an injury arose from the missing/inaccurate information in Autonet's wage statements. See Price v. Starbucks Corp, 192 Cal. App. 4th 1136, 1142-43 (2011). The general demurrer to the third cause of action for violation of Business and Professions Code Section 17200 et seq. is overruled as Plaintiff has sufficiently pled facts regarding Autonet's alleged unfair and unlawful business practices. See Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc., 14 Cal. App. 4th 612, 619, (1993). The general demurrer to the fourth cause of action for breach of contract and/or breach of covenant of good faith is overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts that Autonet breached its oral promise to Plaintiff to repay his deferred salary if he continued to work for Autonet on a reduced salary through its liquidity crisis. (FAC 8, 25.) Plaintiff's special demurrer is overruled as to the first, third, and fourth causes of action. As to the first and third causes of action, Plaintiff is not required to allege the existence of a contract at the pleading stage to overcome a demurrer. As to the fourth cause of action, it can be ascertained from the First Amended Complaint that the alleged contract between Roettgers and Autonet was an oral agreement. (FAC 8, 25.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions For Autonet'S Failure To Appear At Three Party Witness Depositons; And Imporper Instructions At The Continued Deposittions
Off Calendar May-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance: John L. Fitzgerald, Esq. of Strazulo Fitzgerald LLP (415.394.9500), appearing for AUTONET MOBILE, INC., defendant and opposing party; No other appearance.

The tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF CHRISTIAN ROETTGERS' Motion For Sanctions For Autonet's Failure to Appear at Three Party Witnesses Depositions and For Improper Instructions at the Continued Deposition is Denied. The record clearly reflects plaintiff had agreed to move the deposition dates of the three party witnesses upon which Defendants relied. Plaintiff failed to provide evidence that the deposition of Ms. Bonetti was left open as to any and all questions; the transcripts fails to set forth clearly the full reason for a further deposition but it is clear there were certain objections that were made the basis for the further deposition. Further plaintiff did not provide a CRC 3.1345(a) statement as required. Sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Arlene Barton; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Apr-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BARBARA GIUFFRE OF WORK/ENVIRONMENT LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 981-9114 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT AUTONET MOBILE, INC.'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. THIS MATTER IS PRESENTLY SET FOR TRIAL ON MAY 20, 2013 AND PLAINTIFF HAS THE RIGHT TO COMPLETE THE TAKING OF HIS DEPOSITION TO PREPARE FOR TRIAL. THERE HAS BEEN NO SHOWING OF GOOD CAUSE AS REQUIRED BY SECTION 2025.420(b) TO JUSTIFY THE ISSUANCE OF A PROTECTIVE ORDER. MR. PRATZ HAS HAD ALMOST ONE MONTH TO OBTAIN SEPARATE COUNSEL IF HE HAD SO CHOSEN TO DO SO AND HIS FAILURE TO DO SO IS NOT A BASIS FOR DENYING PLAINTIFF THE RIGHT TO COMPLETE THE DEPOSITION. IF THE COURT GRANT'S PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT AND ADD MR. PRATZ AS A NAMED DEFENDANT UNDOUBTEDLY THE PRESENT TRIAL DATE WILL BE VACATED AND COUNSEL SHOULD BE ABLE TO WORK OUT A SCHEDULE THAT WILL ACCOMMODATE THE INTERESTS OF ALL PARTIES TO THIS LITIGATION. THE COURT WILL NOT IMPOSE ANY MONETARY SANCTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Paul Renne, Judge Pro-Tem, Cynthia Herbert, Clerk, Not Reported. =302/JPT
